original_image_tags: Black-and-white photograph of a propeller-driven aircraft, the Vought V-173 "Flying Flapjack," in flight. The aircraft has a wide, disc-like fuselage with short, stout wings and two propellers at the wingtips. It is shown at an angle, with landing gear retracted. The caption is partially visible below the image.

elements.description: A black and white photograph showing a single twin-engine, mid-wing aircraft (Navy's 'Flying Flapjack') in flight with its landing gear retracted. Below the image is the text 'Acme Telephoto'. Further below is a headline 'Can This Be the Secret?' followed by a caption describing the aircraft as a possible solution to the 'saucer' mystery. At the bottom of the clipping is a date stamp 'JUL 6 1947' and the text 'WASHINGTON TIMES-HERALD Page 1 & 8 Sec. I'.

judge.reason: Both sides agree on the main visual content: a black-and-white photograph of the Vought V-173 'Flying Flapjack' in flight with landing gear retracted; Description B adds specific textual elements (e.g., 'Acme Telephoto', headline, date stamp) not mentioned in A, but these are not contradicted.
